criteria.label: Choose a set for uniqueness and fast membership; recall its O(1) operations.
criteria.example: Dedupe URLs and check membership? `seen = set(); 'url' in seen` is O(1). Same with a list it's O(n) per check — at 1M URLs you'd burn minutes. Set is the right tool whenever you ask 'have I seen this before?'

criteria.label: Use |, &, -, ^ for union, intersection, difference, symmetric difference.
criteria.example:
```python
a, b = {'x','y'}, {'y','z'}
a | b   # {'x','y','z'}  union
a & b   # {'y'}          intersection (overlapping tags)
a - b   # {'x'}          difference (only in a)
a ^ b   # {'x','z'}      symmetric difference (in exactly one)
```

criteria.label: Dedupe order-sensitively with dict.fromkeys (or a seen-set), not list(set(...)).
criteria.example: `list(set(['a','b','a','c']))` loses order (and can vary across runs). `list(dict.fromkeys(['a','b','a','c']))` → `['a','b','c']`, order preserved (dict is insertion-ordered since 3.7).

criteria.label: Pick frozenset when a hashable, immutable set is needed (dict keys, set members).
criteria.example: Need a set as a dict key (e.g. caching by feature flags)? `cache[frozenset({'fast','no_color'})] = result` works; a plain `set` would raise `TypeError: unhashable`.

criteria.label: Distinguish {} (dict) from set(); avoid relying on set iteration order.
criteria.example: `x = {}` makes an empty *dict*, not an empty set — use `set()` for that. And while dicts preserve insertion order, sets do *not* — never iterate a set if you need stable order; use a list or `sorted()`.
